title: Boolean
subtitle: True or False
goal: Learn how JavaScript stores true and false values.
explanation: Boolean is a data type that can hold only two values: true or false.
It is used when the answer can only be "yes" or "no".
For example: is the door open or closed, is the user logged in, is the product available.
Boolean is very important for conditions and decision-making in a program.
Almost every if statement checks a boolean value.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Boolean is like a light switch. It can only be in two states: on or off.

remember: Boolean is used for program logic.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Do not confuse the boolean true with the string "true". The first is a logical value; the second is plain text.

quiz.question: How many values does the Boolean type have?
